items.caption:
Okay why is she so good at this??

#joymoop @joymoop_official what did ya think? 😋💅🏻
items.ownerFullName: Morgan Graffis | Cleaning Business Coach | Hiring & Growth
items.transcript: So at 67, we know I've mopped a ton of floors. I don't clean any more full-time. It's just for my personal use. And look how cute it is. And look, it goes with my toenails. They're purple. Nice big head so that I'll be able to get it done quick. Come from the good old day when you underquoted everybody and had to get it done in one quarter of the time. Okay, one thing is I don't know how to get these little sticks out, so I probably should have read the directions. Oh, look, they just come out this way. I think. Oh, yeah, they do. How do I get in there? I have huge hands. Oh, they came with two extra things. I think this would just pull off. Well, that was pretty easy. I was afraid to break it. There's two extra mop heads, so there's three come with it. Hopefully it has pitchers because I'm not good unless I have pitchers. Oh, look, it even has a pinch handle. Well, I guess I didn't do that right, did I? Okay, what did I do wrong? Surely I need... Oh. I'm not an engineer, that's for sure. When in doubt, just look at the directions, right? Okay, so see this little hole right here? Just clips right on there. And I am 5'9", so I need a long stick. It's adorable. This side is your tank of water, and this is the dry. It says to put it in here until it's wet. I guess it helped if I turn it the right way. To your desired wetness. I think that would be it. So then you put it in the dry one, and you do it to your desired dryness. I might go one more since it's wood floors. They said you could do it up to like six times. So easy. Glides super easy. No stiffness, no sticking. It's very soft and smooth. So that was pretty dirty. So this just washes off the debris, which is great. And then just bring it back through here. I think I'm going to do... I liked it about two, three times. I think I'll... Three.

items.ownerFullName: Morgan Graffis | Cleaning Business Coach | Hiring & Growth

items.caption:
You hire me to do your hiring and sales 😋💅🏻

If you’re a cleaning company owner who wants to grow your business, comment Done-For-You and I’ll send you the info 🥰

#cleaners
items.ownerFullName: Morgan Graffis | Cleaning Business Coach | Hiring & Growth
items.transcript: What exactly happens when you hire me to do sales and hiring in your cleaning company? Step number one is onboarding. So you're going to fill out an onboarding questionnaire where it takes through everything about you and your business that we need to know to hire for you and to do sales for you. You're going to get set up with two account managers. One is going to be in charge of hiring in your business and one is going to be in charge of sales in your business. They're going to ask you any clarifying questions about the onboarding questionnaire that you filled out. That way they're completely set up to start taking over hiring and sales for you. Step two is you get access to the accelerator program. You might have these systems in place already in your business and if so that's fantastic, but you might not. So when we hire for you and we find good people to bring into your company, how are you going to train them? That's a system that you need to have in place for sure and it's included in the accelerator program. How are you going to make sure that you are profitable and charging your clients the right amount to be able to afford a cleaning team? Definitely need to have that system in place. So that's going to be in the accelerator program. When we book walkthroughs for you, how are you going to actually conduct those walkthroughs or create those quotes? That's definitely a system you need to have in place. That's in the accelerator program. Step number three is your account managers actually execute your sales and hiring strategy. Your sales account manager will be executing a sales campaign for you each and every week. Those sales campaigns range from warm and cold outreach to finding and finalizing influencer partnerships to putting sales campaigns together for you like giveaways, promotions of any sort, email marketing campaigns, sometimes even in-person events, and your hiring account manager is in charge of promoting the jobs that you have open in your company. So maybe you've got a general manager position open in your company, lead position open in your company, cleaner position open in your company. Whatever that looks like, they're going to be promoting the job that's actually open in your business right now. They're also in charge of going through all of the applicants and seeing who's qualified and who isn't. Once they narrow down the qualified applicants, they're in charge of booking the interviews. Once they've booked the interviews, they're in charge of conducting the interviews, and they're also in charge of deciding who moves forward in the hiring process once they've completed the interview. If you're interested, comment done for you and I'll send you the information.

items.caption: Updated and in bio :) #cleaners
items.ownerFullName: Morgan Graffis | Cleaning Business Coach | Hiring & Growth
items.transcript: So this video got a little bit of traction, and I don't even know why because I don't even like this video. I think the video kind of sucks, honestly, that I made. But it is talking about my bidding chart, which is like a quoting tool for house cleans. So 300 people used this quoting tool roughly from the comments on this video and then the comments on the Instagram videos. Anyway, 300 people that downloaded the bidding chart basically had a bunch of questions about how to use the bidding chart because it was a spreadsheet. So this is what it used to look like. I loved this thing. I love spreadsheets. I live in spreadsheets, but not everybody else does. So a lot of questions came from this bidding chart tool. And anyway, long story short, three years later, I have updated the bidding chart finally into a legit software. So now it looks like this. And basically, you just plug and chug. So you put in the rooms that you want to quote. You put in the rest of the specs of the house. You put in add-ons that you want. And if you basically want to change anything about this, the whole thing is customizable, and it stays customized for whatever you want the tool to look like. You can pop it then on your website. So I know a lot of house cleaners like to kind of weed out the price shoppers. And you can customize the pricing too. So it's like on a sliding scale, and you can customize the price of each individual thing on there, or you can just leave it at the default pricing that it is right now. So yeah, it's really cool. Then you can also use the dashboard, which is like the back end of the software. I'll show you that. Essentially what that is, is it keeps track of all of your quotes that you've put out there. So whether somebody put like a little quote, like a self-quoting thing on your website, like they filled it out themselves because they were curious what their pricing was for their house, it'll save that on your end so that you can track who filled that out, and then you can like text them. You can also track any quotes that you've personally done on the quote tool. So like if you are, let's say, on a walkthrough, and then you use the quote tool to figure out what the price is for that house, it'll save it on the dashboard so you can keep track of basically where all of your potential clients are in your specific sales cycle. So anyway, all that to say there's an update, and it's free. There's a link in my bio if you want the updated version of the bidding chart.

items.caption: Day in the life of a cleaning company consultant 😀 does anyone care? 😂
items.ownerFullName: Morgan Graffis | Cleaning Business Coach | Hiring & Growth
items.transcript: Come along with me on the day in the life of a cleaning company consultant. If you're like, what is a cleaning company consultant? Basically, I had a cleaning company myself. I sold that cleaning company and now I do sales and hiring for other cleaning companies. And if you're like, I want to do that too, first, are you looking for a job? Because I am currently hiring for a sales and partnership coordinator, so hit me up if you want to do this too. Anyway, so first and foremost, we get our coffee order. Thank you so much. See you soon. Waking up and trying to enjoy my coffee, we promptly get hit by tornado weather because it's tornado season here in Missouri. So for my first work block of the day, I send out an email blast with a freebie that I created, like a little quote tool for any cleaning companies to use if they want to. I have two more meetings this afternoon. I've got one with my employee and one with a done for you student starting at 5. So I should be done with meetings at about 6.15. So I'll be hitting, by the time I get home, a 12 hour day today, which is typical. But at this point in the day is where I'm about to tap out. So we're going to take our coffee like somebody else would be taking Adderall. Let's do this though. We got this. No, that is not the vibe. I said maybe like fish thing, like when her first pool date, like she's going to give me something. But then she finally opened up. Yeah, no, it's OK. We'll pass on that one.
